checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?
checks.guidance: Cross-check the result against explicit user notes, critique corrections, and watch items. Flag anything the implementation ignored, contradicted, or only partially covered.

findings.detail: Checked the user’s markdown-rendering scope addition against the revised plan and the repo. This part is now explicitly covered: the plan adds a shared `AssetDescription` component and says it will replace the plain-text description blocks in both `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:166-169` and `banodoco-website/src/pages/Resources/ResourceModal.tsx:186-189`, which matches the user note and closes the earlier gap.

The revision diverges from the user’s stated runtime/deliverable shape in a way that still needs to be flagged. The brief asked for two Supabase Edge Functions plus pg_cron, with a promoter function directory under `supabase/functions/`, and a resumable per-channel cursor table; v2 replaces the promoter with PL/pgSQL and removes the cursor table entirely. That may be a pragmatic simplification, but it is still a requirements change rather than a straight implementation of the approved shape.

prior_findings.detail: The user’s added scope note about markdown-rendering resource descriptions is only partially reflected in the plan. The repo does have the exact plain-text `ResourceModal` block the note calls out at `banodoco-website/src/pages/Resources/ResourceModal.tsx:186-189`, but the plan never adds a concrete step for that rendering change or the requested success criterion; it only mentions using the existing markdown renderer for comment content.

One of the explicit edge cases in the brief, attachment removal on Discord edit, is still unresolved in the plan. The proposed schema for `asset_comment_media` only has `(comment_id, media_id, sort_order)` and no visibility/deleted flag, so the plan’s note that it 'could mark `is_deleted` on junction' is not actually backed by any migration or follow-up step.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?
checks.guidance: Look for logic errors, invalid assumptions, broken invariants, schema mismatches, or behavior that would fail at runtime. When the fix adds a conditional branch, check whether it handles all relevant cases — not just the one reported in the issue.

findings.detail: Checked the revised type mapping against the repo and that specific defect is fixed. The plan now maps Discord resource posts to `workflow` or `lora`, which matches the website’s real resource enum in `banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13` and the current filter/card branches that key off `workflow`.

The replacement for FLAG-001 is still incomplete. V2 no longer uses `created_at > cursor`, but it still limits discovery to `discord_messages.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days'`; a thread created 61+ days ago that only later reaches 5 reactions would still never import, even though the product rule says any qualifying OP becomes an asset and the ongoing sync is not scoped to recent posts only.

The plan’s failure-handling story for missing members is not technically sufficient in SQL form. It says an FK miss will be 'logged and skipped', but the proposed promoter logic is a set-based `INSERT ... SELECT` inside one PL/pgSQL function; if one row violates the `members(member_id)` FK, PostgreSQL aborts the statement unless the plan introduces row-level exception isolation, so one bad author can block the whole promoter run rather than just one asset/comment.

prior_findings.detail: The cursor strategy in the promoter is not technically correct for the stated trigger. `discord_messages.reaction_count` is a denormalized field that gets recomputed from `discord_reactions` by the `sync_message_reactors` trigger (`supabase/migrations/20260319100000_fix_sync_reactors_trigger.sql:1-22`), so an older thread can cross the 5-reaction threshold long after its `created_at`. A promoter that only scans `created_at > cursor` will permanently miss those late-promoted OPs once the cursor advances past their creation time.

The fallback member-write described in Phase 2 would violate the current schema. `members` is the renamed `discord_members` table, and its original definition still requires `username TEXT NOT NULL` (`supabase/migrations/20251101000000_create_discord_tables.sql:38-64`); the archive writer in `brain-of-bndc/src/common/storage_handler.py:190-209` also always supplies a username. The plan’s 'insert only member_id, username=NULL' fallback would fail the FK-prep path instead of rescuing it.

The proposed asset type mapping (`lora` or `tool`) does not match the website’s current resource model. Manual resource creation only offers `lora` and `workflow` (`banodoco-website/src/pages/SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`), and the cards/filters branch on `asset.type === 'workflow'` (`ResourceModal.tsx:151-156`, `ResourceCard.tsx:151-154`, `useResourceFilters.ts:159-160`). Importing Discord rows with `type='tool'` would render them as LoRAs in some places and make them unfilterable in others.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?
checks.guidance: Look at how the changed function is used across the codebase. Does the fix only address one caller's scenario while others remain broken? Flag missing required work or out-of-scope edits. A minimal patch is often right, but check whether the underlying problem is bigger than what the issue describes.

findings.detail: Checked the prior surface-scope problems against the revised repo references and those specific fixes are in place at the plan level. V2 moves the primary UI work to `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx`, which is the actual routed surface, and it explicitly threads `is_hidden` through `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, and `useUserProfile` instead of leaving the kill switch modal-local.

The discussion/media interaction work is still broader than the plan acknowledges. `ResourceDetail` currently only swaps `activeMedia` inline (`banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:59-74, 228-245`) and the resource area has no existing comment lightbox component to reuse; the plan nevertheless says comment thumbnails will open an 'existing lightbox' anchored to the parent comment. That means a non-trivial new modal/state/navigation surface is still missing from the scoped implementation steps.

prior_findings.detail: The plan scopes the frontend work around `ResourceModal`, but `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` only finds the component definition and no callers. The live resource surface is `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx`, which still renders `resource.description` as plain text at lines 166-169 and already owns the visible 'Made with this' gallery. Limiting the change to the dead modal leaves the actual resource page behavior unchanged.

The broader 'resource visibility' surface is larger than the plan accounts for. Resource lists and profile counts currently come from `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, `useCommunityResource.ts`, and `useUserProfile.ts`, all of which filter only on `admin_status` and never consider a future `assets.is_hidden` kill switch (`useResources.ts:51-74`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-127`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-113`, `useUserProfile.ts:125-133`). If `is_hidden` is meant to hide imported assets globally, the change needs to reach those callers too, not just one modal/card.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?
checks.guidance: Search for all instances of the symbol/pattern being changed. Also ask: does this feature require setup, registration, or integration code beyond the core logic? Missing glue code causes test failures even when the core fix is correct.

findings.detail: Checked the previously missing glue paths and that part of the revision is covered. The plan now explicitly updates `useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, and `useUserProfile`, so the earlier missing `source`/`discord_channel_id`/`is_hidden` propagation problem is addressed at the plan level.

The Cloudflare reuse story is still incomplete against the actual repository code. `supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-webhook/index.ts:210-240` only writes `cloudflare_playback_hls_url`, `cloudflare_thumbnail_url`, and `storage_provider`; it does not persist duration, even though the revised plan and success criteria say the existing webhook will populate HLS, thumbnail, and duration for imported videos. That supporting-infrastructure contract is still inaccurate unless the plan adds a webhook change or drops duration from the promise.

The new vault bootstrap step is not yet self-contained enough to satisfy the plan’s own `supabase db reset` criterion. There is no existing `vault.create_secret`, `internal.get_service_role_key`, or secret-seeding pattern anywhere under `supabase/`, and the proposed migration depends on an out-of-band `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key_value', true)` value that the repo never sets. As written, the bootstrap is still an infrastructure gap rather than a ready migration recipe.

prior_findings.detail: The plan does not thread the new asset metadata through the existing data loaders that would need it for the requested UI. `useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, and `useCommunityResource.ts` currently select neither `source` nor `discord_channel_id` nor `is_hidden` (`useResources.ts:51-65`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-124`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-109`), so the proposed 'From Discord' badge and hide switch cannot work until those queries and their TypeScript shapes are expanded.

The media-import plan also skips over existing Cloudflare support that already owns part of this lifecycle. The repo has `cloudflare-stream-webhook` for writing HLS/thumbnail URLs from a Stream UID and `cloudflare-stream-backfill` for retrying unfinished ingests (`supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-backfill/index.ts:35-114`), but the new plan introduces a separate importer that polls Cloudflare to readiness itself. That duplication is a supporting-infrastructure gap unless the plan explicitly says which path becomes canonical and how the existing webhook/backfill jobs are reused or retired.

The cron registration snippet assumes a secret-delivery pattern that is not present elsewhere in the repo. Existing pg_cron usage schedules local SQL directly (`supabase/migrations/20260403000005_schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql:4-23`), and `supabase/config.toml:45-46` only contains a commented `db.vault` stub. A migration that references `current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key')` without first establishing that setting will not be self-contained.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?
checks.guidance: Grep for call sites. For each caller, check what values it passes — especially edge cases like None, zero, empty, or composite inputs. Then ask: should this change be here, or in a caller, callee, or new method?

findings.detail: Checked the prior caller propagation defect against the revised plan and that part is addressed. The plan now explicitly revises the four existing resource-consuming hooks (`useResources`, `useCommunityResources`, `useCommunityResource`, `useUserProfile`) so the real callers that feed cards, detail pages, and profile counts would at least receive `source`, `discord_channel_id`, and `is_hidden`.

I also checked the concrete caller that would need to host the new interaction model. `ResourceDetail` currently has only `activeMedia`/`setActiveMedia` state and no comment-modal or lightbox caller boundary (`banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:59-74`), while `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` still shows no imports/usages for the modal. The plan’s promise of an 'existing lightbox' anchored to a parent comment therefore still lacks a real caller path in the current codebase.

prior_findings.detail: I traced the current callers that consume resource rows, and they all pass through the existing hooks with narrow assumptions about the payload: `useResources.ts` reads `id, type, name, description, admin_status, ...`, `useCommunityResources.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, lora_link, ...`, and `useCommunityResource.ts` reads `id, name, description, type, ...` (`useResources.ts:51-65`, `useCommunityResources.ts:118-124`, `useCommunityResource.ts:103-109`). None of those callers request `source`, `discord_channel_id`, or `is_hidden`, so the downstream badge, jump-link, and hide logic proposed in the plan would have no inputs unless the callers themselves are revised.

I also checked the direct callers of the UI surface the plan wants to edit. `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` returns only the component definition and no imports/usages, which means there are currently no callers passing assets into that modal at all. The meaningful caller path today is the route-driven `ResourceDetail` page, so the proposed fix is landing at the wrong boundary unless the plan also restores or introduces actual `ResourceModal` usage.

flags.concern: Discord resource promotion: late-threshold coverage is still incomplete because the revised rolling 60-day scan misses threads older than 60 days that reach the reaction threshold later.
flags.evidence: The revised plan replaces the created_at cursor with `created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days'`, but `discord_messages.reaction_count` is still updated after message creation by the trigger in `supabase/migrations/20260319100000_fix_sync_reactors_trigger.sql:1-22`.

flags.concern: Discord media sync infrastructure: the revised Cloudflare/vault reuse story is still incomplete against the actual repository contracts.
flags.evidence: `supabase/functions/cloudflare-stream-webhook/index.ts:210-240` updates HLS URL and thumbnail but not duration, while the revised plan says the existing webhook will populate duration too; the repo also has no existing `vault.create_secret` or `internal.get_service_role_key` pattern under `supabase/`.

flags.concern: Discord promoter execution: the revised SQL-native promoter still lacks row-level error isolation, so one missing-member FK or other bad row can abort the whole sync instead of being logged and skipped.
flags.evidence: The plan's promoter remains a set-based `INSERT ... SELECT` inside one PL/pgSQL function, but PostgreSQL aborts the statement on FK violation unless the implementation adds per-row exception handling or prefilters invalid rows.

flags.concern: Discord resource discussion UI: the revised frontend plan still assumes an existing comment lightbox/navigation surface that does not exist in the current caller path.
flags.evidence: `banodoco-website/src/pages/ResourceDetail/index.tsx:59-74` only manages inline `activeMedia` state, and `rg -n "ResourceModal" banodoco-website/src` still shows no callers for the modal component.
